reasoning: While the LLM fidelity score is high (0.9182), the programmatic audit identifies two major defects: the fabrication of table structures not present in the source and a 77% failure rate for image rendering. Because this document is identified as a map and chart-heavy file, the loss of visual assets and the hallucination of table structures represent significant risks to document integrity and accessibility.

concerns.description: Fabricated tables: baseline has 0 tables but output has 2
Image placeholder ratio 77%: 10/13 images are broken/missing (transparent 1x1 placeholders)

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is inconsistent throughout the document, particularly concerning sibling sub-headings. In several sections, sub-headings that are stylistically identical in the source (parallel items) are assigned different levels in the output. For example, in Section 14 ('Collaborate for Data Quality and Data Sharing'), 'Statewide Parcels' is an H3 while 'Statewide Addresses' and 'Statewide Roads' are H4s. Similar issues occur in Sections 8, 9, and 12 where siblings vary between H3 and H
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Parallel blue bold sub-headers on pages 28-29: 'Statewide Parcels', 'Statewide Addresses', 'Statewide Roads', 'Census', etc.
concerns.output_quote: Section 14 contains H3 for 'Statewide Parcels' (element index within section) followed by H4s for 'Statewide Addresses', 'Statewide Roads', 'Census', and 'First-Stop...'.

concerns.analysis: Several URLs clearly visible in the source document are not rendered as clickable links in the output. For example, on page 25, the website addresses 'www.ncgicc.org' and 'www.nconemap.com' are rendered as plain underlined text without 'href' objects. Similar issues occur with URLs on pages 4, 15, and 20.
concerns.output_quote: The text is present but lacks an associated 'href' property/object to make it a functional link.

action_items: Verify the content of the two fabricated tables against the source document to check for hallucinations
Restore missing maps, charts, and diagrams identified as placeholders
Standardize the heading hierarchy for Section 14 and similar sub-sections
Convert plain text URLs into functional hyperlinks
